WHILE NOT REQUIRED, PLAINTIFFS ARE ENCOURAGED TO ALLEGE RELEVANT FACT ASSERTED TO SUPPORT ELEMENTS OF A CAUSE OF ACTION IN THE BODY OF EACH CAUSE OF ACTION INSTEAD OF ALLEGING ALL FACTS IN THE GENERAL ALLEGATIONS SECTION. SUCH PLEADING MAY AVOID ANY FUTURE DEMURRER FOR FAILURE TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION. JUDGE: VEDICA PURI; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Not contested. The court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS PETER ISKANDAR, JUSTIN YONKER AND DANIEL WIDJANARKO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend to allege facts in support of each cause of action as to each defendant in clear and concise language. While not required, Plaintiffs are encouraged to allege relevant fact asserted to support elements of a cause of action in the body of each cause of action instead of alleging all facts in the general allegations section. Such pleading may avoid any future demurrer for failure to state a cause of action. Judge: Vedica Puri; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
